title: RabbitMQ 4.3 Delayed Retries, Priorities, and Consumer Timeouts · Boundary Failure Triage
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective RabbitMQ 4.3 feature and fallback compatibility plan; preserve message identity and timing, locate the first broken ownership boundary, and retain a deterministic regression case. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in RabbitMQ 4.3 feature and fallback compatibility plan and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggin…
objectives: Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum delayed-retry policies for returned or failed deliveries with explicit delay, eligibility, attempt, and compatibility evidence.
Compare native delayed retries with TTL and DLX cycles by rewrite cost, loss and duplicate windows, head-of-line effects, topology, and version support.
Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum 32 strict priority levels with bounded use, starvation evidence, prefetch interaction, and per-priority monitoring.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Delayed Retry Policy
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug messaging team handles case 8394 in a quorum queue during a planned rolling maintenance window. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum retry, priority, and timeout behavior on version and valid budgets; allow exactly one terminal acknowledgement decision owner; then change one routing segment and prove the exact destination set. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum delayed-retry policies for returned or failed deliveries with explicit delay, eligibility, attempt, and compatibility evidence.

Inspect the Delayed Retry Policy trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Delayed Retry Policy.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use rabbitmq 4.3 quorum delayed-retry policies for returned or failed deliveries with explicit delay, eligibility, attempt, and compatibility evidence. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Delayed Retry Policy, keep Delayed Retry Policy connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feature Version Gate
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: rabbit-ts-feature-version-gate-ubwfmz" that uses case 3300 to gate RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum retry, priority, and timeout behavior on version and valid budgets. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import amqplib or Node built-ins, open sockets, contact RabbitMQ, Docker, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the named Node 24, amqplib 2.0.1, RabbitMQ 4.3, TLS, partition, restart, load, upgrade, and production transfer gates.

Competency probe: Gate 4.3-only behavior on deployed broker, queue type, policy support, client behavior, migration state, and tested fallback.
